description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a light blue fuselage and tan upper and lower wings, featuring roundels on the wings and a wooden propeller at the nose. The aircraft has a classic open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing, and the tail section is adorned with a red, white, and blue striped rudder. The wings are connected by a series of struts and wires, typical of early 20th-century aircraft design. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The engine is a radial type, partially exposed at the front, giving the plane a distinctive historical appearance.
description: A vintage biplane with tan wings, blue fuselage, open cockpit, and a wooden propeller, featuring classic roundels and a striped tail rudder.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden propeller, radial engine, and symmetrical biplane wings with roundels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open cockpit, blue fuselage, and the striped rudder at the tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, striped rudder, and the alignment of the biplane wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cockpit, wing struts, and the fixed landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; rudder is striped.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
